title: Liu, Lv et al. 2023 — EEG datasets of stroke patients (motor imagery)
description: An EEG motor imagery dataset from 50 acute stroke patients (1-30 days post-stroke) performing left- and right-handed hand-grip imagination tasks. The dataset comprises 40 trials per subject (20 left, 20 right) recorded with a 29-channel wireless EEG system at 500 Hz, including raw and preprocessed data. This is the first open dataset to address bilateral motor imagery in acute stroke patients, providing a critical resource for brain-computer interface algorithm development and clinical rehabilitation applications.
methods_description: EEG data were collected from 50 acute stroke patients using a ZhenTec NT1 wireless multichannel system with 29 EEG channels and 2 EOG channels at 500 Hz sampling rate. Participants performed 40 motor imagery trials (20 left-hand, 20 right-hand grip imagination) guided by video and audio cues. Each 8-second trial consisted of an instruction phase, 4-second video-guided imagery phase, and rest period. Data were preprocessed using EEGLAB (MATLAB R2019b) with FIR filtering (0.5-40 Hz bandpass) and baseline removal. Electrode montage followed the 10-10 system with semi-dry Ag/AgCl electrodes referenced to CPz.

funding_references.funder_name: National Natural Science Foundation of China
